panel_title: Share your perception of nature's biodiversity!
intro:
Welcome to the WASP (World Archives of Species Perception) Project. In this survey, we show images of animals and six rating questions associated with them, and we ask you to rate it, according to your impression of the species — regardless of whether or not you know the species in the image.

Every time you complete six rating questions and press Rate, a response is collected and a new species image will be loaded. We have a total of 1,980 animals for you to discover. You can stop and continue at your own pace.

We request you to complete at least 25 animals, but if you want to explore more, you are absolutely welcome! For doing 25 animals, you'll only need a maximum of 10 minutes.
questions_intro: How would you rate this animal in terms of it being:
why_body: This is a survey being done across the globe. Each species rated adds to the global mapping of human perception for biodiversity. Together, we can generate a global, open-access database for scientists to understand how human perception can help reinforcing biodiversity conservation.
what_body:
For more information about the project: www.wasp-project.net

If you have any questions, please reach out to Jithin Vijayan (University of Turku, Finland; jithinvjyothis@gmail.com), or Dr. Tuan Nguyen (Hasselt University, Belgium; tuan.nguyen@uhasselt.be).
note: 🍪 Note: We use cookies to help you track your progress. Because of this, you can stop in between, and resume the survey at your own pace. If you like this survey, please consider sharing it around! Your contributions can help promote better and wider biodiversity conservation.
cta_label: Ready? Click below to begin the survey.
